title: calendula arvensis proles sublanata
intro: Calendula arvensis proles sublanata, also known as field marigold, is a subspecies of the calendula arvensis plant, a type of annual or biennial herb in the daisy family. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of calendula arvensis proles sublanata include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and soothing skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Calendula arvensis proles sublanata can be prepared for medicinal use through infusion, decoction, or cream, and is often used topically to take advantage of its soothing and healing properties.
